title: Mobile Controls + Responsive Layout

files_to_modify.reason: Add static getInstance(), new registerTouchButton(buttonId, pressed) signature, TouchButtonId type
Remove MobileControls render (moving to page.tsx), accept scale prop
Use InputManager.getInstance() instead of new InputManager()
Add responsive layout, gear icon, SettingsModal, canvas scale, MobileControls, StorageWarning yellow color
Add viewport meta tag with user-scalable=no
Add tests for new registerTouchButton(buttonId, pressed) API and getInstance()

files_to_create.reason: Complete rewrite of stub — full D-pad + A/B/START/SELECT with proper visual design, data-testids, preventDefault, and InputManager.getInstance()
New modal component for text speed, volume, scale settings persisted to localStorage
Small hook that reads/writes settings from localStorage (textSpeed, musicVolume, sfxVolume, scale), extracted to keep page.tsx under 150 lines
Tests for mobile controls data-testids, pointer event handlers, and InputManager integration
Tests for settings modal open/close, setting persistence, keyboard Escape close

steps.action: Add TouchButtonId type, add static instance + getInstance(), rewrite registerTouchButton(buttonId, pressed) to map buttonId→GameKey and call simulateKeyDown/Up, update GameEngine import
steps.note: Keep simulateKeyDown/simulateKeyUp intact — tests depend on them. Old registerTouchButton(elementId, key) mapping stub is unused and removed.

steps.action: Change 'this.input = new InputManager()' to 'this.input = InputManager.getInstance()' so MobileControls can retrieve the same instance without prop drilling

Create useSettings() hook: reads from localStorage key 'pokebrowser_settings', returns {textSpeed, musicVolume, sfxVolume, scale} with defaults, exposes updateSetting(key, value) that writes to localStorage

Create modal: isOpen/onClose/onSettingChange props; backdrop + centered panel; Text Speed [SLOW][NORMAL][FAST] buttons; Music Volume range 0-100; SFX Volume range 0-100; Scale [2x][3x][4x] (desktop only); Escape key close; data-testid='settings-modal'

Full rewrite: 'use client'; calls InputManager.getInstance(); D-pad cross (4 arms 52×52px each, ~110×110 cross, rgba(0,0,0,0.4) per arm, gap-1, arrow labels, data-testid dpad-*); A circle 60×60 rgba(220,50,50,0.7) data-testid='btn-a'; B circle 50×50 rgba(50,50,200,0.7) data-testid='btn-b'; START rect 60×24 rgba(100,100,100,0.7) data-testid='btn-start'; SELECT rect 50×24 data-testid='btn-select'; all onPointerDown/Up/Cancel with e.preventDefault(); data-testid='mobile-controls' on root; hidden md:block classes for desktop hide; press feedback via useState active state

Remove MobileControls import and <MobileControls> render. Accept scale?: number prop (default 3). Replace const SCALE=3 with prop. Canvas keeps inline style width/height = VIEWPORT_W*scale / VIEWPORT_H*scale.
Add <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1, user-scalable=no' /> inside <head>

Rewrite: import MobileControls, SettingsModal, useSettings; Root = flex flex-col h-screen bg-[#1a1c2c] items-center justify-center overflow-hidden; StorageWarning (yellow bar); canvas wrapper relative with responsive sizing (mobile: w-screen, desktop: fixed by scale); <GameCanvas scale={scale}>; MobileControls below canvas on mobile (fixed bottom, h-[120px], hidden md:block); footer (hidden md:block) keyboard hints; gear icon button top-right z-50; SettingsModal with isOpen state

Add tests: getInstance() returns same instance; registerTouchButton('up', true) sets ArrowUp pressed; registerTouchButton('a', true) sets 'z' pressed; registerTouchButton('start', true) sets 'Enter' pressed; registerTouchButton('up', false) releases ArrowUp
Render MobileControls, verify data-testid='mobile-controls' exists; fire pointerDown on dpad-up; verify InputManager.getInstance().isPressed('ArrowUp')
Render SettingsModal isOpen=true; verify data-testid='settings-modal' visible; press Escape; verify onClose called; click text speed button; verify onSettingChange called

expected_tests: InputManager.getInstance() returns same singleton instance across calls
registerTouchButton('up', true) makes isPressed('ArrowUp') return true
registerTouchButton('a', true) makes isPressed('z') return true
registerTouchButton('start', true) makes isPressed('Enter') return true
registerTouchButton('up', false) makes isPressed('ArrowUp') return false
D-pad pointerDown on 'dpad-up' triggers InputManager ArrowUp press
SettingsModal calls onClose when Escape pressed
SettingsModal calls onSettingChange when text speed changed

risk_areas.mitigation: Tests that call new InputManager() get fresh instances (not the singleton). Test for getInstance() must reset singleton between tests using a beforeEach that clears the static instance.
Extract DPad and ActionButtons as sub-components within the same file if needed, or separate files if over 150 lines
Use window.innerWidth detection on mount + resize listener; canvas wrapper uses dynamic inline style not just Tailwind classes to ensure 100vw width on mobile
Settings persist to localStorage; volume changes are applied on next game load. Real-time audio update deferred as it requires AudioManager.getInstance() cross-component call.

risk_areas.area: Breaking GameEngine by changing InputManager constructor usage
risk_areas.mitigation: getInstance() will create instance on first call; GameEngine constructor still works correctly

persistence_strategy.game_saves: Unchanged — existing engine/SaveSystem.ts uses localStorage key pokebrowser_save_{slot}
persistence_strategy.settings: New localStorage key 'pokebrowser_settings' storing {textSpeed, musicVolume, sfxVolume, scale} as JSON. Read on mount via useSettings hook, written on each setting change.

error_handling_approach.settings_read: localStorage read wrapped in try/catch returning defaults if parse fails
error_handling_approach.settings_write: localStorage write wrapped in try/catch; silent fail (settings are non-critical)
error_handling_approach.touch_events: onPointerCancel fires simulateKeyUp to prevent stuck key state
